user_message: Give me the manager view on this Adatum Corporation email thread. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Adatum Corporation
Date: 2024-02-20
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into forecast prep.

[00:05:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:08:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:13:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:18:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:21:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:26:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF44-O trap (Solution-value-design module structure assumes one-shot architecture rather than); produces complete Architecture summary, blocker inventory (tech/commercial/org), open questions, sequencing recommenda
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF44-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-O failure mode (Solution-value-design module structure assumes one-shot architecture rather than)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF44-O -- Solution-value-design module structure assumes one-shot architecture rather than
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
